title: Metanarrative review of documentation as a social phenomenon in early childhood education and care
abstract: Documentation is an increasingly central part of all societal activities. In early childhood education and care (ECEC), documentation and record keeping are used to secure children's well-being, participation and rights. Although documentation is a much-studied topic, the conceptual understanding of this phenomenon, which is intertwined in the lives of all children in many ways, remains thin. In this study, we applied the metanarrative review protocol to the analysis of the definitions given to the documentation of previous studies (N = 124). As a result, we summarised the conceptual approaches of ECEC documentation into four narratives that describe documentation as (1) a meaning-making process, (2) a practice of professional reflection, (3) an outcome-focused solution and (4) a complex and problematic phenomenon. The overarching definition of documentation calls for acknowledging the interpretability and consequentiality of the phenomenon and the danger of a simplistic idealisation of its benefits.
authors_text: Heiskanen, Noora; Poikola, Mirva; Franck, Karianne
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
